**Türk Telekom Basketbol Kulübü** ([English](/wiki/English_language "English language"): Turk Telekom Basketball Club) is a professional [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") team that is based in [Ankara](/wiki/Ankara "Ankara"), [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"). The team is currently playing in the [Turkish Basketball Super League](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Super_League "Turkish Basketball Super League"). It is a branch of the [Türk Telekom GSK](/wiki/T%C3%BCrk_Telekom_GSK "Türk Telekom GSK") sports club. Their home arena is the [Ankara Arena](/wiki/Ankara_Arena "Ankara Arena"). The arena, which has a capacity of 10,400 seats, opened in April 2010.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=T%C3%BCrk_Telekom_B.K.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9f/T%C3%BCrk_Telekom_logo.svg/250px-T%C3%BCrk_Telekom_logo.svg.png)](/wiki/File:T%C3%BCrk_Telekom_logo.svg)

Türk Telekom logo used till 2016

The club was founded in 1954 and opened its basketball section as name of PTT in 1991. Thereafter, the team received name of Türk Telekom PTT in 1996. Recently, the team is continuing with the name of Türk Telekom.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Türk Telekom has won the [Turkish Cup](/wiki/Turkish_Cup_Basketball "Turkish Cup Basketball") title after beating [Oyak Renault](/wiki/Oyak_Renault "Oyak Renault") in 2008. Also, the team won the [Turkish President's Cup](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_President%27s_Cup "Turkish Basketball President's Cup") title after beating [Fenerbahçe Ülker](/wiki/Fenerbah%C3%A7e_Men%27s_Basketball "Fenerbahçe Men's Basketball") that year.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Türk Telekom also has a reserve team as name of Genç Telekom. They compete in the [Turkish Basketball Second League](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Second_League "Turkish Basketball Second League").

In September 2011, first [Turkish](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey") [NBA All-Star](/wiki/List_of_NBA_All-Stars "List of NBA All-Stars") player [Mehmet Okur](/wiki/Mehmet_Okur "Mehmet Okur") of the [Utah Jazz](/wiki/Utah_Jazz "Utah Jazz") announced he would play for Türk Telekom, due to the [2011 NBA lockout](/wiki/2011_NBA_lockout "2011 NBA lockout").

In [2016](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Basketbol_S%C3%BCper_Ligi "2015–16 Basketbol Süper Ligi"), Türk Telekom relegated from the BSL after finishing 15th in the regular season. In 2018, the team returned as it promoted from the [Turkish First League](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_First_League "Turkish Basketball First League").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In its first season back, the club would also play in the [EuroCup](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_EuroCup_Basketball "2018–19 EuroCup Basketball"), as the club returns to Europe after 6 years.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The club played in the finals of the [2022–23 EuroCup Basketball](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_EuroCup_Basketball "2022–23 EuroCup Basketball") season and achieved the runners-up position.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
